core_problem: My dad is experiencing forgetting to pee every 2 hours despite needing to during all times and everywhere (except during sleep), which leads to health issues.

problem_details.problem: Needs to pee every 2 hours but always forgets
problem_details.when_where: All the time, everywhere (except during sleep)
problem_details.impact: Health issues (affects recovery from BPH surgery)
problem_details.frequency_requirement: Every 2 hours (medically prescribed, only during waking hours)

medical_context.condition: Recovery phase after surgery for benign prostatic hyperplasia (BPH)
medical_context.requirement: Active peeing every 2 hours is required to exercise the bladder
medical_context.underlying_issue: Bladder has problems due to long-time benign prostatic hyperplasia
medical_context.notes: Regular 2-hour intervals are medically necessary for bladder rehabilitation

tech_stack.deployment: Direct APK installation (no app store distribution)
tech_stack.rationale: Critical for medical app reliability - native APIs provide best background notification reliability and DND override capabilities

required: Platform-native notification APIs (no third-party push services needed for local notifications)
avoid: Third-party notification services (not needed for local notifications)

core_components: Timer Manager - Manages 2-hour timer logic, state transitions, retry mechanism
Alarm Service - Schedules and cancels exact alarms using AlarmManager.setExactAndAllowWhileIdle()
Notification Service - Platform-specific notification scheduling and delivery
Reminder Receiver - Handles alarm triggers and shows notifications
Boot Receiver - Reschedules alarms after device reboot
Storage Service - Persists app state (sleep mode, timer state, settings)
UI Layer - Display information, handle user interactions (MVVM pattern)
key_interactions: App Launch → Load state → Calculate next reminder → Schedule alarm → Schedule notification
Alarm Trigger → ReminderReceiver → Check sleep mode → Display notification → Schedule retry alarm
Retry Alarm Trigger → ReminderReceiver → Show retry notification
User Acknowledges → Reset timer → Cancel retry alarm → Schedule next reminder alarm → Update UI
Retry Mechanism → 15-minute alarm → Retry notification → Handle cascade prevention
Sleep Mode Toggle → Cancel all alarms → Update state
Device Reboot → BootReceiver → Load state → Recalculate → Reschedule alarms

critical_bottlenecks: Background notification reliability - Android WorkManager + AlarmManager.setExactAndAllowWhileIdle() (Android 11+)
DND override - Android varies by manufacturer, requires battery optimization exemption
Timer state persistence - Need reliable local storage + recovery mechanism
Retry mechanism state tracking - Multiple timer states require careful state machine design

cost_intensive: Device testing: $200-500 for Android 11+ test devices (if not available)
Note: Direct APK installation, no app store accounts needed

scalability_concerns: Single-user design - No scalability concerns for MVP (by design)
Timer accuracy across long periods - Need to recalculate on app launch
Battery optimization aggressiveness - Android devices vary, may require user configuration
Platform fragmentation - Different Android manufacturers have different behaviors
decisions_log: Decision 1: Problem defined - Reminder system needed for 2-hour intervals during waking hours only
Decision 2: Solution selected - Smartphone app (Solution 1) for MVP
Decision 3: Manual sleep toggle for MVP (no automatic detection)
Decision 4: Local-only storage for MVP (no cloud sync)
Decision 5: Hybrid timer approach - follows user dismissal with fallback retry mechanism
Decision 6: Override DND mode - health reminders always work (prevents accidental blocking)
Decision 7: Acknowledgment button hidden by default, can be enabled in settings
Decision 8: Native Android development chosen over cross-platform (React Native/Flutter) for reliability
Decision 9: Android 11+ (API 30+) target - uses Jetpack Compose + WorkManager + AlarmManager.setExactAndAllowWhileIdle()
Decision 10: MVVM architecture pattern
Decision 11: SharedPreferences for MVP storage (can upgrade later)
Decision 12: Direct APK installation - no app store distribution needed
Decision 13: AlarmManager.setExactAndAllowWhileIdle() for reliable background reminders (Hour 5)
Decision 14: BootReceiver for alarm recovery after device reboot (Hour 5)
Decision 15: AlarmService wrapper class for centralized alarm management (Hour 5)
Decision 16: MainViewModel for UI state management with countdown timer updates (Hour 6)
Decision 17: MainScreen composable with large, accessible UI elements for elderly users (Hour 6)
Decision 18: SettingsScreen with acknowledgment button visibility toggle (Hour 7)
Decision 19: Simple state-based navigation between MainScreen and SettingsScreen (Hour 7)
Decision 20: Onboarding completion marked after first permission request (Hour 7)
Decision 21: All unit tests passing - TimerManager, NotificationService tested (Hour 8)
Decision 22: Fixed deprecation warnings - using Icons.AutoMirrored.Filled.ArrowBack (Hour 8)
current_blockers: Need to clarify timer recovery behavior after long device off period
Need to clarify sleep mode toggle behavior during active reminder
requirements_document: Requirements.md (v1.0.1) - Finalized and approved for MVP development
architecture_document: ARCHITECTURE-AUDIT.md (v1.0.0) - Technical audit and architecture design complete
project_plan: PROJECT-PLAN.md (v1.0.0) - 6-phase development plan with timelines and risk assessment
mvp_8hour_plan: MVP-8HOUR-PLAN.md (v1.0.0) - Aggressive 8-hour MVP development plan with hour-by-hour breakdown

project_timeline.ai_acceleration: 40-60% time savings on code generation, architecture, UI, and documentation
phases: Phase 1: Project Setup & Foundation (2-3 days, LOW risk, 60% AI acceleration)
Phase 2: Core Timer Logic (1-2 weeks, MEDIUM risk, 50% AI acceleration)
Phase 3: Notification System (1.5-2.5 weeks, HIGH risk, 40% AI acceleration)
Phase 4: UI Implementation (1-2 weeks, MEDIUM risk, 60% AI acceleration)
Phase 5: Integration & Polish (1-2 weeks, MEDIUM risk, 30% AI acceleration)
Phase 6: Testing & Deployment (3-5 days, LOW risk, 20% AI acceleration)
project_timeline.note: Physical device testing and integration debugging still require significant human time
